title: Renderer console + runtime error scan (HBR-VIS-002)
goal: Per spec §6.4.3 + HBR-VIS-002, every captured frame must include a renderer console/runtime error scan. Uncaught errors during the test window fail the gate. Implement the scan as a Playwright fixture that subscribes to CDP `Runtime.consoleAPICalled` + `Runtime.exceptionThrown` + Playwright `pageerror` events for the test window's duration.
expected_diff_shape: New test fixture tests/visual/console_error_scan.ts; updated webview2_cdp_driver.ts (MT-021) to wire the scan into every screenshot test; sample failing fixture proves the scan catches an injected error.
implementation_notes: Per spec §6.4.3 (AC-VISUAL-DEBUGGER-IPC) the scan combines three event sources: CDP `Runtime.consoleAPICalled` (filtered to level in {error, warning}), CDP `Runtime.exceptionThrown`, and Playwright's high-level `page.on('pageerror', ...)`. The fixture: `class ConsoleErrorScan { constructor(page: Page); subscribe(): void; assertNoErrors(): void; }`. subscribe() registers all three listeners and accumulates events in an in-memory buffer. assertNoErrors() throws if the buffer has any error-level event during the test window. Integration with the Playwright test API: provide a Playwright fixture `consoleScan` that auto-subscribes at test start and auto-asserts at test end (via the test.afterEach hook), so existing tests pick it up by importing the fixture. Per HBR-VIS-002 evidence_kind=renderer_console_error_report the test output produces a JSONL log of every error event seen (for forensic review even on passing tests). console_error_scan.spec.ts contains two tests: (1) happy-path navigates to the main app and asserts no errors; (2) deliberate-error injects `await page.evaluate(() => { throw new Error('inject-test'); })` and asserts the scan caught it and the test fails as expected (use Playwright's `test.fail()` decorator). The scan respects HBR-VIS-002 not_applicable_when by skipping when HBR-VIS-001 is N/A for the WP.
proof_commands: cd app && pnpm exec playwright test tests/visual/console_error_scan.spec.ts
risk_if_missed: Without renderer error scan, visual tests can pass while the UI is throwing uncaught errors invisibly; HBR-VIS-002 has no evidence.
validator_focus: Both deliberate-error test fails (the scan caught it) AND happy-path test passes (the scan does not false-positive on normal app load); JSONL forensic log is produced for every test run.

builder_handoff_correction_20260525.operator_instruction: Operator clarified that completed/pass-like states were not validator-reviewed and instructed Kernel Builder to mark everything ready for validator handoff except the blocked item.
builder_handoff_correction_20260525.current_interpretation: Kernel Builder implementation handoff readiness only; no WP/Integration Validator pass or validator-reviewed completion is claimed.

lifecycle.ready_for_validation_reason: Kernel Builder believes implementation/supporting evidence is ready for Integration Validator handoff; validator review remains pending.

minimum_controls: Scan subscribes to all three event sources (not just one) so a regression in any one source cannot blind the scan.
JSONL forensic log is produced even on passing tests for post-hoc review.
test.fail() decorator confirms the scan can produce a failure when needed (not just a no-op).

integration_validator_review.verdict_reason: ConsoleErrorScan subscribes to all three event sources (page.on('pageerror'), CDP Runtime.consoleAPICalled, CDP Runtime.exceptionThrown), buffers events with structured schema_id, writes a JSONL forensic log per test, exposes assertNoErrors() that throws on any error-level event (and optionally warnings); installed as an auto Playwright fixture so every visual test picks it up.

integration_validator_review_prior.verdict_reason: Renderer console + runtime error scan does not exist; HBR-VIS-002 'every captured frame must include a renderer console/runtime error scan' has no implementation surface.
